senses.definition: a preparation of growth products of the fungus Blastomyces dermatitidis, historically used to test for blastomycosis
examples: Historically, clinicians used blastomycin in skin tests to detect blastomycosis.
The use of blastomycin as a diagnostic reagent is now largely obsolete.

senses.definition: plural of blastomycosis; fungal infections caused by Blastomyces dermatitidis
examples: Blastomycoses can affect the lungs and skin.
In many regions, blastomycoses are more common in outdoor workers.

senses.definition: an infectious disease caused by the fungus Blastomyces dermatitidis, typically affecting the lungs and skin
examples: Blastomycosis is contracted from environmental exposure to the fungus.
Symptoms of blastomycosis may include cough and skin lesions.

senses.definition: relating to blastomycosis or caused by Blastomyces dermatitidis
examples: Blastomycotic lesions were observed on the patient’s arm.
A blastomycotic infection requires antifungal therapy.

senses.definition: a term that appears to be a compound or specialized anatomy word (not widely attested in common dictionaries)
examples: The blastoneuropore was described in the embryology notes.
Further literature is needed to confirm the usage of blastoneuropore.

senses.definition: a genus name in biology (phytophagous insects or related taxa); used as a scientific term
examples: Species of blastophaga are studied in entomology courses.
The blastophaga fertilization process was described in the paper.

senses.definition: relating to blastophores or a blastophore-like feature in embryology
examples: The blastophitic cells were observed during gastrulation.
Researchers noted blastophitic movements in the specimen.

senses.definition: pertaining to a blastophore, an opening formed during early development
examples: Blastophoral slits were described in the embryological diagram.
The blastophoral region shows invagination during development.

senses.definition: an opening or invagination in early embryonic development, such as the blastopore
examples: The blastophore marks the beginning of gastrulation.
Cells move through the blastophore during development.

senses.definition: relating to a blastophore or to a blasting or opening feature
examples: Blastophoric activity was noted in the specimen study.
The blastophoric mechanism is essential in gastrulation.

senses.definition: a rare or obsolete term in embryology referring to blastophthory or related blastophore phenomena
examples: blastophthoria is rarely used in modern texts.
The historian cited blastophthoria in old manuscripts.

senses.definition: relating to blastophthoria or blastophoric processes
examples: The blastophthoric process was described in the text.

senses.definition: a genus name in botany; a group within the plant kingdom
examples: Blastosophyllum is studied in a course on plant morphology.
The specimen belongs to the blastophyllum genus.

senses.definition: an anatomical term referring to a pore or opening associated with blasts or blastopores
examples: The blastopor was observed in the specimen under microscopy.
Researchers described an unusual blastopor in the tissue section.

senses.definition: relating to a blastopore or an opening in embryology
examples: Blastoporal development is a key stage in embryogenesis.
The blastoporal region shows early cellular movements.

senses.definition: an opening in an embryo that forms during gastrulation, such as the blastopore
examples: The blastopore marks where the embryo begins to form the gut.
Cells move toward the blastopore during early development.

senses.definition: plural of blastopore; openings formed during embryonic development
examples: Blastopores appear as the embryo undergoes gastrulation.
Different species have varying numbers of blastopores.

senses.definition: relating to or resembling a blastopore
examples: The blastoporic region showed rapid folding.
Blastoporic mechanisms are described in the developmental literature.

senses.definition: pertaining to blastoporphyrite or blastoporphyritic features (rare/technical term)
examples: The blastoporphyritic layer was noted in the specimen.

senses.definition: plural or misspelled form; likely intended as blastopores or a typo
examples: Blastopors is probably a misspelling of blastopores.

senses.definition: a term referring to a sphere of blastomeres or a developmental cell mass in embryology
examples: The blastosphere comprises several embryonic cells.
Researchers examined the blastosphere under the microscope.

senses.definition: plural of blastosphere; multiple embryonic cell spheres
examples: The blastospheres were cultured in vitro.
Different developmental stages produced several blastospheres.

senses.definition: relating to a blastosphere or blastospheres
examples: Blastospheric divisions were evident in the developing embryo.

senses.definition: a spore formed by blastomycotic or related processes; a wall-bound spore
examples: The blastospore was identified in the fungal culture.
Researchers studied blastospores for pathogenicity.

senses.definition: plural of blastospore; a type of spore produced by some fungi
examples: Blastospores are a form of fungal spore.
Laboratories may observe blastsospores under a microscope.
